findings.detail: verify-run.json records three runs: (1) test: exit_code=0, junit total=323, failed=0, skipped=0. The 56 new test cases cover Sb1593c-specific paths: dql.test.ts (30 unit), extract.test.ts extractTasks (6 unit), query.spec.ts (11 acceptance), cli-query.spec.ts (7 acceptance). (2) lint: exit_code=0. (3) ui-tests: exit_code=0, junit total=111, failed=0, skipped=0. Sb1593c-2 E2E tests appear in the JUnit case list: dataview.e2e.spec.ts all 3 AC tests plus 5 mock tests from dataview.mock.spec.ts. verification-results.json author reports the same numbers (434 total, 0 fail, 0 skip) and reconciles each AC to specific JUnit rows. No discrepancy found between machine ground truth and model-authored report.

findings.ac: POST /api/query DQL サブセット実行 + 400 位置情報
findings.detail: Route defined in packages/server/src/routes/search.ts line 57: app.post('/api/query', ...). It calls parseQuery(body.data.query) then executeQuery(parsed, index.queryNotes()). DqlParseError is caught and returned as 400 with {error:'query_syntax', message, line, column, length}. parseQuery is a full hand-written tokenizer+parser in packages/shared/src/dql.ts supporting LIST/TABLE/TASK, FROM #tag|"folder", WHERE with =, !=, >, <, >=, <=, contains, AND, bare/negated truthy, SORT field [asc|desc]. Position info is 1-based line/column/length on every token. executeQuery is a pure function. The acceptance test tests/acceptance/query.spec.ts makes real HTTP calls (not mock) and verifies concrete user-observable assertions including 400 status + exact line:1, column:6, length:4 fields for the LIST form #reading case. TABLE, LIST, TASK, file.name/folder/mtime, contains+and, and empty query rejection tested against a real server+vault.

findings.ac: タスクインデックス化 + TASK クエリ + 外部編集追従
findings.detail: extractTasks() in packages/shared/src/extract.ts uses TASK_RE regex matching -/*/+/1./1) + [ ]/[x]/[X]. It skips frontmatter and fenced code blocks via scannableLines(). VaultIndex.refreshFile() in packages/server/src/noteIndex.ts stores tasks: extractTasks(content). VaultIndex.queryNotes() includes tasks in QueryableNote. Three update paths exist: build() full-walk, indexSyncMiddleware (write-through), chokidar watcher. The acceptance test query.spec.ts tests all four scenarios: TASK !completed with line numbers and indent, TASK completed with indent:4 for a nested task, write-through (API append then immediate TASK query), and external fs.writeFile with pollUntil for chokidar follow. All four pass in verify-run.json JUnit.

findings.ac: loamium query コマンド API 1:1 + --json + 構文エラー非0 + stderr JSON
findings.detail: packages/cli/src/main.ts registers sub('query', ...) with .argument('<dql>', ...). Action calls apiFetch(base, '/api/query', postJson({ query: dql })). Human output: LIST→one path per line, TABLE→tab-separated header+rows, TASK→path:line: [x/space] text. --json calls printRaw(result). Syntax errors from the server (400) propagate as CliError and reach fail('query_syntax', err.message, 1) via the error chain, writing {error, message} to stderr. No-argument case handled by Commander's exitOverride→fail('usage',...,2). cli-query.spec.ts runs real binary subprocess (runCli) with LOAMIUM_URL pointing at a real server, checking stdout content, stderr JSON parsing, and exit codes. All 7 tests pass.

findings.ac: dataview フェンス描画 LIST/TABLE/TASK + クリックナビゲーション
findings.detail: packages/ui/src/renderers/dataview.ts registers a fence renderer for lang='dataview' with mode='replace'. renderDataview() calls api.query() then dispatches to renderList/renderTable/renderTasks. dataview-widget[data-query-type], dataview-item[data-path], dataview-task[data-path][data-line] testids are all set. Navigation uses mousedown+preventDefault+stopPropagation (wireNavigation) calling ctx.env.openNote or ctx.env.openNoteAtLine. The E2E test packages/ui/tests/e2e/dataview.e2e.spec.ts uses NO page.route/MSW/fetch mocking — it calls putNote() which is a real fetch to the actual API, then uses real Playwright browser against real Vite+server. It verifies: widgets with correct data-query-type, list items with data-path, task item with data-path and data-line, item click navigates breadcrumb, task click moves activeLine. Confirmed no page.route in dataview.e2e.spec.ts. Mock tests with page.route are correctly isolated to dataview.mock.spec.ts.

findings.ac: vault ファイル変更追従でクエリ結果更新
findings.detail: renderDataview() in packages/ui/src/renderers/dataview.ts sets a setInterval at DATAVIEW_REFRESH_MS=2000ms. On each tick it calls runOnce(code, ctx), compares JSON.stringify(res) as signature, and replaces children only when signature changes. el.isConnected check clears the interval when the widget leaves DOM (no memory leak). The E2E test is real: it uses putNote() to add a new #dvproj note via the real API and waits up to 10s for items count to change from 2 to 3. Then it adds a task line and waits for task count 1→2. This exercises the full path: real API write → server indexSyncMiddleware → queryNotes() → polling diff detected → DOM update.

findings.ac: 構文エラーのフェンス内表示 + エディタ編集継続
findings.detail: renderQueryError() in dataview.ts builds a <pre data-testid='dataview-error'> with caret visualization (spaces*(col-1) + carets*length). Widget gets data-query-type='error'. The E2E test puts a note with 'LIST form #reading', opens it, verifies dataview-widget[data-query-type=error], checks dataview-error contains exact strings 'クエリを解析できません (400)', 'LIST form #reading', '^^^^', '1 行 6 列: 予期しないトークン \'form\''. Then verifies editor is still editable (types text, checks it appears). Then clicks the error widget and verifies it disappears (cursor moves to fence source). All assertions on user-observable DOM/text with no mocks.

Scanned git diff main...HEAD for: (1) it.skip/test.skip/xit — none found in package source. (2) expect(true).toBe(true) or weakened assertions — none found. (3) empty catch {} — the only catch blocks in new code are in noteIndex.ts handling expected file-gone cases (not swallowing meaningful errors). (4) @ts-ignore — none found in diff. (5) 'as any' or ': any' — none found in diff (code uses unknown+narrowing throughout). (6) ROADMAP.json AC text: the diff only changes status fields and counters — no AC text was removed or loosened. The AC blocks for Sb1593c-1 and Sb1593c-2 remain untouched.

Checked against DESIGN_PRINCIPLES.json forbidden list: (1) Block IDs written to files — not done; dataview renderer is display-layer only (mode:'replace'), no vault writes. (2) Note lines converted to block arrays — not done. (3) Obsidian-incompatible custom syntax — dataview fence uses standard code fence (```dataview) which Obsidian ignores gracefully as an unregistered fence language; does not break plain Markdown. (4) Plugin API — not created. (5) vault root external reads/writes — not done. (6) Bulk delete/convert without confirmation — not done. (7) Test invalidation/weakening — not done. (8) ROADMAP AC deletion/loosening — not done. The priority-1 rule (pure Markdown: no writes from renderer to vault files) is honored: renderDataview calls api.query() for reads only, never any PUT/POST to notes.

decisions.json records 7 planning decisions and 8 implementation decisions. Key compromises explicitly documented: (I5) polling at 2s interval instead of SSE/WebSocket push — justified as SSE infrastructure would be a major out-of-scope addition; polling self-stops via el.isConnected. (I8) fence-bar dynamic summary omitted (static info string) — AC does not require this. (R2) readFile→stat race in refreshFile — judged self-healing via next chokidar event, accepted. (R3) efficiency: extractTasks/extractTags/extractLinks each call parseNote separately — accepted for simplicity. All compromises are scoped to non-AC behavior or self-healing patterns. No AC was silently dropped. done_judgment in verification-results.json marks all 8 guards pass/n/a for both stories.
